detail: Normal 2026 / Elevated 2027 / High from 2028. Resource additions lag escalating demand forecasts and announced retirements; ~18 GW of data-center load projected by 2035.
Elevated 2026-2028 / High from 2029. Demand growth driven primarily by data centers; anticipated resource margin falls below Reference Margin Level starting 2029.
Elevated 2026-2028 / High from 2029. Rapid load growth outpaces additions in later years; ERCOT granted authority to curtail new large loads in grid emergencies.
Elevated 2026-2028 / High from 2029. Load growth and retirements against a predominantly solar-PV addition mix; summer unserved-energy risk.
Normal 2026-2028 / High from 2029. Rapid demand growth; unserved energy projected in both summer and winter.
Elevated. Demand forecasts outpace resource additions; low-wind/high-outage scenarios show energy shortfall risk.
Elevated. Winter natural-gas infrastructure limitations pose extreme-condition shortfall risk.
Elevated. Peaking-generator retirements create localized adequacy needs (NYISO 2025 Q3 STAR report).
Elevated. Resource additions lag demand forecasts and retirements; shortfall risk in below-normal winter temperatures.
